title: Ideator concepts.json — the Ideator→Writer contract
description: A ranked slate of comic concept pitches produced by the ideator's concept tournament (Stage 1). This is the artifact the Writer (Stage 2) consumes: it reads selected_concept_id and expands that concept into a panel-ready script. Built to docs/PRODUCTION-SYSTEM-VISION.md §4. Scores are produced against skills/ideator/references/rubric.md; growth/story fields are grounded in research/comic-corpus findings.

schema_version.description: Version of THIS contract. Start at 1.0; bump on any breaking field change.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the slate was produced.

seed.description: The spark this slate was generated from: a theme, character, phrase, or null for 'surprise me'.

rubric_version.description: Version of references/rubric.md the scores were produced against.

corpus_synthesis_version.description: Which research/comic-corpus synthesis the grounding/findings cite (e.g. 'success-elements v2').

roster_snapshot.description: The locked characters available for reuse at generation time (cheaper to produce — refs already exist).

concepts.description: The full slate (typically >= 8, >= 2 per generation angle). Ranked via 'ranking'.

ranking.description: concept_ids ordered best -> worst by weighted_total.

top3.description: The three concept_ids surfaced to the user at the human gate.

selected_concept_id.description: The concept the user picked at the human gate. Null until selection. The Writer reads THIS.

logline.description: One sentence — the whole comic in a breath.

premise.description: A short paragraph: who, the situation, what changes.

flavor.description: The growth mechanic, e.g. potion | curse | latent-power | tech | rivalry-driven | ambient-field | training | infection.

trigger.description: The catalyst beat that starts the growth.

arc.description: The growth spine: trigger -> escalation -> peak -> aftermath, as a one-line beat string.

peak_state.description: What the transformed peak looks like (the money state).

tier_curve.description: Optional est. tier escalation, in the pipeline's peak-body-scale vocabulary (e.g. 'tier 3 -> 7').

cast.description: Named cast only (no background extras, per house rule). Prefer locked characters for cheaper production.

role.description: e.g. protagonist | rival | mentor | catalyst | antagonist.

reuse.description: True if this is an existing locked character.

ref_status.description: locked = refs exist; new = a full ref pack must be built (a production cost).

cast_size.description: Derived: number of named cast members.

setting.description: Where it happens; note if an env/location ref pack already exists.

hook.description: The scroll-stopping promise — what makes someone open page 1.

est_growth_page_ratio.description: Target share of pages that are active-growth. Corpus Finding 1 targets: transformation >= 0.60, climax >= 0.70, action/plot >= 0.30.

planned_escalation_devices.description: Which corpus growth devices the concept is built to use (Finding 4 steal-list).

generation_angle.description: Which tournament angle produced this concept.

why_itll_perform.description: The rationale: why this lands with the audience, tied to evidence.

corpus_grounding.description: Which corpus findings the concept leans on, e.g. ['F1 growth-ratio-by-intent', 'F5 story-as-differentiator'].

scores.description: Per-axis 0-5 against references/rubric.md. Produced by the (stubbed) scoring engine or by hand.

weighted_total.description: Weighted, normalized score (see rubric weights). Used to rank.

score_rationale.description: Optional short justification per axis or overall.
